title: corydalis pauciflora var. alpestris
intro: Corydalis pauciflora var. alpestris, also known as alpine corydalis, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of alpine corydalis include relief from pain, inflammation, and anxiety, as well as improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. Alpine corydalis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
